text: Welcome to this video concerning total robotic revision of complex gastrointestinal gastric fistula after laparoscopic Roux-en-Y gastric bypass. A forty-eight-year-old woman who had undergone a laparoscopic Roux-en-Y gastric bypass three years ago for class III obesity consulted for wide regain and epigastric pain. An upper gastrointestinal endoscopy and gastrointestinal contrast study show a type II gastro-gastric fistula.

The surgical setup includes six trocars: a 12 mm port for the camera, two 8 mm ports for the robotic arms, two ports (12 mm and 5 mm) for stapling and assistance, and an additional 5 mm port for liver protection.

The surgeon performs adhesiolysis of the left lobe of the liver, small gastric pouch, alimentary limb, and gastrojejunostomy using robotic scissors. A block resection of the anastomosis reveals a complex gastrojejunal gastric fistula.
The surgeon performs adhesiolysis on the proximal gastric bulge, followed by stapling to resize the gastric pouch.

The surgeon sutures the gastro-gastric fistula on the excluded stomach using a contiguous barbell suture technique. An epiplasty is performed to prevent recurrence of the fistula, with visible reinforcement of the tissue.

The surgeon staples the proximal alimentary limb using three stapling devices. A new gastrojejunal anastomosis is created by Hansen, employing a continuous suture technique with absorbable sutures, cut without knots.
Methylene blue stained saline is injected through an orogastric tube to check for leaks, followed by the application of ferritin sealant. No drain is inserted at the end of the procedure.
Operating time was one hundred five minutes. Post-operative course was uneventful and the patient was discharged at day three.
